sourcingNotes.poolAssessment: The eat-hub-hero-01 brief asks for category 5 (dining room interior) with Unsplash → Pexels → venue-media-kit as preferred sources. In this sourcing session, Unsplash and Pexels are both behind anti-bot protection (Anubis proof-of-work challenge on Unsplash, Cloudflare interactive challenge on Pexels). I cannot browse their catalogs to produce a verifiable candidate list with real URLs, photographer names, and licence confirmation. Wikimedia Commons holds almost no editorial-grade empty restaurant interiors and zero that are Peninsula-verifiable venue media-kit outreach has not begun (per sourcing report §4.2, waits until editorial voice is settled).
sourcingNotes.blocker: No honest shortlist can be produced for this slot from this session. Producing one anyway would require me to fabricate candidate URLs or silently use non-whitelist sources, both of which violate the single invariant of the approval workflow.
recommendedUnblockPath: Option 1: Provide an Unsplash access path (API key, headed browser session, or a manually-curated list of Unsplash URLs from the editor). The sourcing report §8.1 already defines the query templates; execution just needs unblocked search.

Option 2: Defer eat-hub-hero-01 to week 2 after venue media-kit outreach begins, and take an early Tedesca / Laura at Pt. Leo / Montalto dining-room image directly from a media kit. This is the highest-fidelity option.
Option 3: Defer eat-hub-hero-01 to Phase 2 commissioned photography. Accept the fog placeholder stays on /V2/eat/ through week 3.

editorActionsRequested.question: Which unblock path should sourcing use for the categorical Unsplash/Pexels-preferred Tier A slots (eat, stay, escape, journal hub heroes)?
editorActionsRequested.defaultIfNoReply: Defer all four slots until venue media-kit outreach and/or Unsplash API access is authorised.
